signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: With its basketball section dating back to 1928, AS Monaco boasts a long tradition in the sport. Monaco's first foray into international competition came with Korac Cup outings in the 1974-75 and 1982-83 seasons. The club dropped as far as the fourth division until a remarkable revival the last few years that led to its current golden period. Monaco made its BKT EuroCup debut in the 2018-19 campaign. Two years later, Monaco secured its first-ever continental title by defeating UNICS Kazan in the EuroCup Finals, as it progressed to the EuroLeague for the very first time. Monaco replaced Zvezdan Mitrovic with Sasa Obradovic during its debut EuroLeague campaign and went on to reach the playoffs in all four of its EuroLeague campaigns to date and made its first Final Four in 2023. Mike James was chosen as the 2023-24 EuroLeague MVP and the club claimed French League crowns in 2023 and 2024. Last season, Monaco hired coach Vassilis Spanoulis and went all the way to its first EuroLeague Championship Game, downing Olympiacos in the semifinals before losing to Fenerbahce in the championship game. The next goal is clear: Coach Spanoulis, James and Nikola Mirotic want to take Monaco all the way to EuroLeague glory in 2026!

heroMedia.title: Monaco rocked by James injury blow
heroMedia.summary: EuroLeague’s all-time leading scorer faces a spell on the sidelines

content.text:
AS Monaco’s playoff hopes have been hit with confirmation that superstar Mike James will miss two or three weeks of action with a hamstring injury.

The all-time EuroLeague scoring leader suffered the injury during the penultimate minute of his team’s dramatic win over Olympiacos Piraeus on Thursday, and the extent of the problem was confirmed by the club after tests on Friday.

James will miss next week’s trip to Anadolu Efes Istanbul and must be considered highly doubtful for the subsequent double round week at the end of March, when Monaco will face a pair of direct rivals in the race for a top ten finish: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan at home, followed by a trip to Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens.

The veteran playmaker leads Monaco in both scoring (16.7 points per game) and assists (6.6 per night), and his absence will place further pressure on an already-undermanned roster which contained just nine players for the victory over Olympiacos.

articleMetadata.title: Monaco rocked by James injury blow

heroMedia.title: Strazel’s late heroics lift shorthanded Monaco over Reds
heroMedia.summary: With key absences and major upheaval, Monaco leans on heart, hustle and late‑game poise to secure a dramatic win

content.text:
There was a strange atmosphere at Salle Gaston Mededin when AS Monaco hosted Olympiacos Piraeus in Round 31. Monaco's [Elie Okobo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/elie-okobo/011212/) and [Nikola Mirotic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-mirotic/000796/) were unavailable due to injury and [Vassilis Spanoulis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/spanoulis-vassilis/?season=E2024), who led the Roca Team to the EuroLeague Championship Game last season, had parted ways with the club two days earlier. [David Michineau](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/david-michineau/003836/) and [Yoan Makoundou](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/yoan-makoundou/011935/) also left Monaco this week. And Olympiacos was without star forward [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/), too. 

Despite these circumstances, Monaco fought hard and served up an 80-79 home victory in epic fashion, overcoming all sorts of adversities to grab its 17th victory of the season. 

"The guys showed up. This was an amazing game," interim head coach [Manuchar Markoishvili](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/markoishvili-manuchar) said after the game. "It was also an amazing experience for me. I cannot ask more effort to this team [after] what they showed by themselves. I am not going to talk about tactics, this was an amazing effort, and their effort decided the game. They hustled and gave all they had, being short-handed."

Monaco's great hustle could be seen in the stat sheet, too. The hosts outrebounded Olympiacos 9-1 in the fourth quarter, forced Olympiacos into 15 turnovers and allowed the Reds just 13 assists. On the other hand, Monaco finished the game with 19 assists and 11 turnovers. [Daniel Theis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/daniel-theis/004132/) led his team's effort with 13 points, 3 offensive rebounds and 3 assists for a PIR of 16 in just 21 minutes. 

"It was a very, very big win, especially with all the circumstances. But we, as players, can only control how hard we play and what we do on the court, and I think we did this tonight. We showed heart and were fighting the whole game, no matter the outcome," Theis said. "Even if we had lost the game on the last shot, we played our heart out. It is a tough situation for us. We are down a lot of players, coaches and like I said, we can only control what we do on the court. We have to play 100% and give it all on the court, win or lose."

As Theis said, the game went down to the final shot. After a back-and-forth battle throughout the fourth quarter, [Alec Peters](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/alec-peters/008829/) tied the game with a layup, 79-79, with 51.7 seconds remaining. Monaco superstar [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) left the game with an injury a few seconds after that. [Nemanja Nedovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nemanja-nedovic/000848/) and [Matthew Strazel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/matthew-strazel/008099/) missed shots and [Alpha Diallo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/alpha-diallo/011226/) fouled Peters, a career 89.1% free throw shooter. He split his 2 attempts, however, and Strazel banked in the go-ahead jumper with 4.0 seconds left. 

Olympiacos called timeout, but Monaco's defense forced [Nikola Milutinov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-milutinov/003941/) to take a rare three-point shot, his third attempt of the season. In 283 career games, Milutinov was 2-of-7 from downtown before this shot, which he missed.

💎🇲🇨 Matthew called game.

Against all odds ✍️💯#RocaTeam #EveryGameMatters pic.twitter.com/agy3s5AKY1

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 12, 2026

"It was unexpected to play with this kind of intensity with the roster we had, but we found a way to push ourselves for 40 minutes and luckily, I had the ball at the end of the game and I am just happy right now," Strazel said after the game. "Whatever happens until the end of the season, that is the face we want to show, and the face we will show." 

James paced the winners with 14 points, 5 rebounds and 5 assists before his injury. [Jaron Blossomgame](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jaron-blossomgame/011231/) added 13 points. Strazel paired 9 points with 5 assists. Monaco plays three of its next four games on the road, starting with a visit to Anadolu Efes Istanbul on March 19, before finishing the regular season with a three-game home stretch.

articleMetadata.title: Strazel’s late heroics lift shorthanded Monaco over Reds
articleMetadata.description: In a turbulent week marked by injuries, roster departures and the exit of Vassilis Spanoulis, AS Monaco delivered a spirited 80–79 victory over Olympiacos in Round 31. Interim coach Manuchar Markoishvili guided a short‑handed team that dominated the fourth‑quarter hustle stats, with Daniel Theis and Matthew Strazel leading a gritty defensive effort. Mike James exited late with injury, but Strazel’s clutch jumper sealed a dramatic win as Monaco prepares for a difficult road stretch.

heroMedia.title: Manuchar Markoishvili takes over as Monaco head coach
heroMedia.summary: Sergii Gladyr will act as his assistant

content.text:
[AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) has confirmed that, following [the departure of Vassilis Spanoulis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/vassilis-spanoulis-parts-ways-with-monaco-as-head-coach/) and his two assistants, Ilias Kantzouris and Kostas Charalampidis, the team will now be led by Manuchar Markoishvili and Sergii Gladyr.

Markoishvili, [who was previously interim coach for Monaco's Round 11 win over LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/news/coach-markoishvili-elie-okobo-enjoyed-a-night-to-remember-against-asvel/), will act as head coach, [the club confirmed on its website](https://asmonaco.basketball/news/show.php?ID=75769).

Monaco sits ninth in the standings with a 16-14 record but is in the midst of a 1-7 skid. Markoishvili's first game in charge comes this Thursday night as Olympiacos Piraeus visits the principality.

Suite au départ de Vassilis Spanoulis, ses adjoints Ilias Kantzouris et Kostas Charalampidis quittent également le club. L'ensemble du club les remercie pour leur engagement et le travail accompli avec l'équipe.

👔 Les responsabilités du coaching seront désormais assurées par… pic.twitter.com/RAPPydasGG

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 12, 2026

"Following the departure of Vassilis Spanoulis, his assistants Ilias Kantzouris and Kostas Charalampidis are also leaving the club. The club thanks them for their commitment and the work they put in with the team," Monaco said in a statement on X.

"Coaching responsibilities will now be handled by Manuchar Markoishvili and Sergii Gladyr, while Sigitas Kavaliauskas retains his role as strength and conditioning coach."

articleMetadata.title: Manuchar Markoishvili takes over as Monaco head coach
articleMetadata.description: AS Monaco has confirmed that, following the departure of Vassilis Spanoulis and his two assistants, Ilias Kantzouris and Kostas Charalampidis, the team will now be led by Manuchar Markoishvili and Sergii Gladyr.

heroMedia.title: Vassilis Spanoulis parts ways with Monaco as head coach
heroMedia.summary: He had a 34-26 record in charge of the Monegasque club

content.text:
[AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) and Vassilis Spanoulis have parted ways by mutual agreement, the club confirmed Wednesday.

Spanoulis arrived at Monaco in late November 2024, replacing coach Sasa Obradovic. He helped lead the club to a fourth-place finish in the regular season and a playoff triumph over FC Barcelona in a five-game series, which guaranteed its second-ever appearance at the EuroLeague Final Four in May 2025.

In Abu Dhabi, [Monaco defeated Olympiacos Piraeus 78-68](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/mike-james-leads-monaco-past-olympiacos-and-into-championship-game-68-78/) in the semifinals to reach its first-ever Championship Game, but [fell short against Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul, losing 81-70](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/fenerbahce-crowned-2025-champion-with-70-81-win-over-monaco/) in the title decider.

Expectations were high for Monaco this season. The team held a strong 15-7 record after 22 EuroLeague games, sitting near the top of the standings. However, a recent collapse has derailed its campaign: Monaco has gone 1-7 over the last eight games and tumbled to ninth place with a 16-14 record. Spanoulis attributed the team's struggles to off-court problems.

He was not on the bench for Monaco's French League win over Cholet Basket this past weekend, nor was his first assistant, Ilias Kantzouris. That was also the case for the team's French Cup victory on Tuesday night. Instead, Sergiy Gladyr led the team.

L’AS Monaco Basket et Vassilis Spanoulis ont convenu d'un commun accord de se séparer, avec effet immédiat.

Le club remercie Vassilis Spanoulis pour la passion, l'énergie et le dévouement dont il a fait preuve au sein de l’organisation. Son engagement envers l'équipe et les… pic.twitter.com/qo6VGKt9lN

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 11, 2026

In the 2024-25 season, Spanoulis compiled an 18-12 record with Monaco. His overall record stands at 34-26 across both seasons.

Under Spanoulis's leadership, Monaco has posted its two best-ever scoring performances in the EuroLeague, both this season: [a 125-104 win over Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/monaco-does-it-again-euroleague-history-made-against-paris/) in Round 14 and [a 107-112 overtime victory at Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/mike-james-lights-up-maccabi-in-ot-shootout-makes-euroleague-history/) in Round 9.

Monaco's three biggest wins in the EuroLeague have also come under Spanoulis. Last season, the club set a record by [winning by 30 points at Fenerbahce (69-99)](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/monaco-made-a-statement-with-huge-win-at-fenerbahce/) in just his fourth game in charge. Monaco then surpassed that mark twice this season in consecutive games: [a 32-point victory at LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (52-84)](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/monaco-keeps-its-foot-on-the-throttle-en-route-to-record-night/) in Round 12 and [a 36-point win against Anadolu Efes Istanbul (102-66)](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/monaco-breaks-club-history-again-in-beatdown-of-efes/) in Round 13.

articleMetadata.title: Vassilis Spanoulis parts ways with Monaco as head coach
articleMetadata.description: AS Monaco and Vassilis Spanoulis have parted ways by mutual agreement, the club confirmed Wednesday.

heroMedia.title: Monaco reaches French Cup final
heroMedia.summary: Six players finished in double figures in points (Photo: L'Equipe)

content.text:
[AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) punched its ticket to the French Cup final with an 83-88 win over Nanterre on Tuesday, leading wire to wire despite the continued absence of head coach Vassilis Spanoulis and his assistant Ilias Kantzouris. The Roca Team will face Le Mans at Accor Arena in Paris on April 25 in a rematch of the Leaders Cup final that Monaco won convincingly 103-79 on February 22.

With [Nikola Mirotic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nikola-mirotic/000796/) sidelined through injury and [Daniel Theis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/daniel-theis/004132/) unavailable due to personal reasons, Monaco was down to just one true center in [Kevarrius Hayes](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kevarrius-hayes/010390/) and leaned on its guards and perimeter speed to get the job done. That approach paid off, and the Roca Team's backcourt came up big when it mattered most, securing what was its 20th straight win against French opposition across all competitions since November 2025.

[Alpha Diallo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/alpha-diallo/011226/) led the way with 16 points, followed by [Matthew Strazel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/matthew-strazel/008099/) with 15, [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) and [Nemanja Nedovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nemanja-nedovic/000848/) with 14 apiece, [Elie Okobo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/elie-okobo/011212/) with 12, and [Jaron Blossomgame](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jaron-blossomgame/011231/) with 10.

🔥 On est en finale de #CDFBasket !!

Merci aux #RocaFans pour votre soutien et félicitations à @Nanterre92 pour une belle bataille 👏#RocaTeam #DagheMunegu 🇲🇨 pic.twitter.com/pX1WyakvuV

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 10, 2026

Monaco controlled the game from the opening tip, taking a 39-45 lead into halftime before exploding out of the locker room with a stunning 19-3 run in under five minutes to push its advantage to 19 points at 42-61.

Nanterre did fight back in the fourth quarter, trimming the deficit to six at 74-80 as Hayes was hampered by foul trouble and their frontcourt found more room to operate. But Monaco's guards steadied the ship and made sure the Roca Team closed it out, booking only its second-ever French Cup final appearance and a shot at a second title after its 2023 triumph.

articleMetadata.title: Monaco reaches French Cup final
articleMetadata.description: AS Monaco punched its ticket to the French Cup final with an 83-88 win over Nanterre on Tuesday, leading wire to wire despite the continued absence of head coach Vassilis Spanoulis and his assistant Ilias Kantzouris.

heroMedia.title: David Michineau exits Monaco
heroMedia.summary: He made just four appearances in the EuroLeague this season

content.text:
[AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) has confirmed the departure of [David Michineau](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/david-michineau/003836/), who has left by mutual agreement.

Michineau, 31, played just four times in the EuroLeague for Monaco, playing a total of almost nine minutes, scoring two points, dishing out two assists and recording one steal.

D'un commun accord, l'AS Monaco Basket et David Michineau ont décidé de mettre fin au contrat du joueur.

🇲🇨 Toute la famille de la #RocaTeam souhaite une bonne continuation à David pour la suite de sa carrière 👏#DagheMunegu pic.twitter.com/w9jo0nsF8A

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 7, 2026

"By mutual agreement, AS Monaco Basket and David Michineau have decided to terminate the player's contract," Monaco said on Saturday.

"The entire #RocaTeam family wishes David all the best for the rest of his career."

articleMetadata.title: David Michineau exits Monaco
articleMetadata.description: AS Monaco has confirmed the departure of David Michineau, who has left by mutual agreement.

heroMedia.title: Talen Horton-Tucker bulldozes Fener past Monaco to celebrate Saras’s birthday in style
heroMedia.summary: THT paired 28 points with 8 boards to inspire the champs' ninth straight victory

linkedIds.text: Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul v AS Monaco (2026-03-05T17:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fenerbahce-beko-istanbul/ulk/) was in control for large parts of its game against [AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) on Thursday evening. And when it was briefly losing composure, [Talen Horton-Tucker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/talen-horton-tucker/014124/) stepped up to make sure that his team had the upper hand.

In a rematch of last season’s EuroLeague Championship Game, Fenerbahce crushed Vassilis Spanoulis’s side 88-70 to extend its winning run to nine games – and 18 in all competitions. An early 9-0 run set the tone for the hosts, who grabbed a 12-4 lead. They held Monaco to 15 points in the first quarter and 16 in the third, outscoring the visitors in each of the four periods by dictating the tempo throughout.

To do so, Fener relied on Horton-Tucker, who opened the second quarter with five straight points to establish a 25-17 cushion and was the only player on the floor to reach double digits in scoring with 10 points before the break, as Fenerbahce secured a 44-36 advantage at halftime.

content.title: Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul-AS Monaco | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
A back-and-forth affair in the opening stages of the second half saw [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) trim the deficit to 53-46. With Fenerbahce looking to absorb Monaco’s comeback attempt, THT came to the fore by netting three straight buckets , including a dunk off a steal in transition that opened a 59-46 lead.

[Nemanja Nedovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nemanja-nedovic/000848/) drilled back-to-back threes that cut the difference to 69-62 in the fourth quarter, but also triggered Horton-Tucker, who responded with eight straight points that allowed Fenerbahce to grab a 77-68 cushion before two triples from [Tarik Biberovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tarik-biberovic/006961/) finished the game off in crunch time.

“Our coaching staff really don’t let us panic,” Horton-Tucker told EuroLeague TV. “They keep us poised. They respond, we’ve got to respond back. If we just continue to follow the game plan that they continue to put in front of us, we’ll be in a good spot.”

ONE MORE. ✅#YellowLegacy #EuroLeague pic.twitter.com/dmr5z2CeI2

— Fenerbahçe Beko (@FBBasketbol) March 5, 2026

On the sidelines, a delighted Saras Jasikevicius had just received the best birthday present he could have asked for: a rare victory on his birthday.

“I don’t think I ever won on my birthday, so this is amazing,” he told EuroLeague TV after the game. “I saw in the stands, during the game, entered my brother and some very good friends, so God save Istanbul tonight.”

Horton-Tucker finished the game on 28 points, just 1 shy of his career high that he recorded last week in a narrow 81-78 home win over Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade. He also corralled 8 rebounds, as well as 3 assists and 2 steals, contributing the most on both ends of the floor. This was a second consecutive outstanding outing against Monaco by Horton-Tucker, who bagged 24 points to inspire an 86-92 comeback win at Salle Gaston Medecin in December.

linkedId.text: Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul v AS Monaco (2026-03-05T17:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
“We just have to keep fighting. Just try to get every win that we can,” he added. “Especially this point of the season, it gets very tough, with teams chasing a playoff spot and things like this.”

Having improved its record to 22-7, Fenerbahce is pushing for a top-four finish that would clinch home-court advantage in the playoffs. And despite missing two important cogs in Devon Hall and Nicolo Melli through injuries, Jasikevicius’s well-oiled machine looks unstoppable at the moment.

“It’s nine in a row wins for us,” Wade Baldwin told EuroLeague TV. "We’re finding ways to win big games, even with the absences of Devon Hall and Nicolo Melli, who are kind of a backbone to our team. Guys are consistently stepping up their performances. This team is just a bunch of fighters and continued to win a very important game today.”

articleMetadata.title: Talen Horton-Tucker bulldozes Fener past Monaco to celebrate Saras’s birthday in style
articleMetadata.description: In a rematch of last season’s EuroLeague Championship Game, Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul crushed Vassilis Spanoulis’s AS Monaco 88-70 to extend its winning run to nine games - and 18 in all competitions.

heroMedia.title: Yoan Makoundou leaves Monaco
heroMedia.summary: He made five EuroLeague appearances this season

content.text:
[Yoan Makoundou](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/yoan-makoundou/011935/) has agreed to mutually terminate his contract at [AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/), the club announced Thursday. 

The 25-year-old big man played just five times in the EuroLeague this season, averaging 6.2 points and 1.8 rebounds in an average of 7:29 minutes per night. This was his second campaign among Europe's elite, having made his debut in 2022-23, when he made eight appearances for Monaco and averaged 4.4 points and 2.8 rebounds per contest.

L'AS Monaco Basket et Yoan Makoundou ont décidé d'un commun accord de résilier le contrat du joueur.

L'ensemble du club souhaite à Yoan le meilleur pour la suite de sa carrière 👏#RocaTeam #DagheMunegu pic.twitter.com/uu1veqx1FV

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 5, 2026

"AS Monaco Basket and Yoan Makoundou have mutually agreed to terminate the player's contract," Monaco stated in a tweet. "The entire club wishes Yoan the best for the rest of his career."

Makoundou returned to Monaco last offseason after two impressive loan spells in the BKT EuroCup, first with Buducnost VOLI Podgorica in 2023-24 and then with Turk Telekom Ankara in 2024-25.

articleMetadata.title: Yoan Makoundou leaves Monaco
articleMetadata.description: Yoan Makoundou has agreed to mutually terminate his contract at AS Monaco, the club announced Thursday.

heroMedia.title: Maccabi holds firm late to defeat Monaco on the road
heroMedia.summary: Walker scores six straight in the clutch as Maccabi claims its fourth straight win

linkedIds.text: AS Monaco v Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (2026-02-26T18:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/maccabi-rapyd-tel-aviv/tel/) boosted its hopes of squeezing into the postseason, improving to 14-15 with an 86-91 win at [AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) thanks to a balanced, composed performance, in which several players stood out to cancel a valiant solo display from the home side’s [Jaron Blossomgame](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jaron-blossomgame/011231/).

The visitors led for the lion’s share of the contest, wiping out an early 11-10 deficit with a 0-11 run and maintaining control until a brief spurt by Monaco, which nosed ahead 76-75 in the fourth quarter before running out of steam during the closing stages.

Blossomgame delivered the best game of his EuroLeague career with 25 points and a PIR of 33, adding 7 boards and 2 assists, but [Alpha Diallo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/alpha-diallo/011226/) was the only other beacon of light in a shorthanded Monaco side, adding 19 points for the hosts. [Matthew Strazel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/matthew-strazel/008099/) and [Elie Okobo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/elie-okobo/011212/) scored 10 each and [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) added 9, but the latter two combined to shoot 1‑of‑12 from deep as they epitomized a dreadful shooting night for the Roca Team, which made a mere 7‑of‑35 from downtown.

Monaco slipped to 16-13 and will now be looking over its shoulders in ninth after a sixth loss in its last seven games.

content.title: AS Monaco-Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
In contrast, a steady Maccabi outfit was led by [Lonnie Walker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/lonnie-walker-iv/013403/) on 18 points, while [Roman Sorkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/roman-sorkin/011219/) and [Jaylen Hoard](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jaylen-hoard/006835/) chipped in with 15 each, the latter also racking up 7 rebounds. [Oshae Brissett](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/o-shae-j-brissett/014127/) added 12 points as the visiting team dished out 22 assists and won the battle of the boards 37-33, hitting 12‑of‑35 from deep.

Coach Oded Kattash stressed it was a morale-boosting win for Maccabi, which kept its nerve in a tense finish as Walker scored 6 successive points to keep Monaco at bay after the hosts had threatened to force the final twist.

“We showed character and it’s a very special win for us in a tough place,” Kattash said in his postgame comments. “It’s tough to play against Monaco defensively and we did a good job, while keeping our focus offensively and it was a team effort. We started the game really well and kept believing in tough moments.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Maccabi Tel Aviv Basketball (@maccabitlv)

Hoard, who carried Maccabi offensively in the first half when he netted 13 points, was just as upbeat as he summed up the Israeli side’s campaign spent far from home due to security conditions that forced the team to host games in Belgrade in a largely empty gym. He also emphasized that Maccabi has worked hard to hold on in crunch time after suffering several tough losses this term.

“Throughout the season we’ve been in games like this where we let the lead slip away and lost, but we’ve learned from our mistakes in the past and were able to get the win today,” he said. “The chemistry is bringing us closer and closer together each and every day after some hard times we’ve been through.”

Having thrived at one point when it was top of the table, Monaco has plunged down the standings and its campaign is threatening to spiral out of control as pack leader James seems to be struggling, while coach Vassilis Spanoulis tried to put on a brave face after yet another unconvincing display.

“We were out of energy as we only had 11 players available after having to play five games in a week, but it’s not an excuse,” the Greek tactician said. “They played better, so congratulations to them for the win. The first period was a disaster for us as we conceded 30 points. The next three periods we played well, but once again our defense was not good as we conceded (in excess of) 90 points.”

linkedId.text: AS Monaco v Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (2026-02-26T18:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Maccabi holds firm late to defeat Monaco on the road
articleMetadata.description: Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv earned a crucial 86–91 road victory at AS Monaco to extend its winning streak to four and move to 14–15. Despite a career night from Monaco’s Jaron Blossomgame (25 points, PIR 33), Maccabi controlled most of the game and sealed the win behind Lonnie Walker’s late scoring burst. Roman Sorkin and Jaylen Hoard added 15 each, while the visitors won the rebounding battle and knocked down 12 threes. Coaches Oded Kattash and Vassilis Spanoulis reflected on their teams’ contrasting trajectories in the playoff race.

content.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season

heroMedia.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season

content.text:
*EuroLeague & Friends Global* returns for its second episode, once again hosted by four‑time EuroLeague champion Kyle Hines. This time, the show dives into *"Crown Season"*, a new docu-series created by Anadolu Efes Istanbul guard [Shane Larkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/shane-larkin/007200/). Kyle is joined by Larkin along with two of the series’ guest stars: [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/), AS Monaco’s playmaker, and [Mathias Lessort](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mathias-lessort/003842/), Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens’ center.

The conversation explores the insights and untold stories highlighted in the series — from their personal investments to Mathias’s road to recovery, plus a behind‑the‑scenes look at their lives away from basketball. Mathias shares his feeling on how he will feel when he finally plays a EuroLeague home game after a long recovery process The players also reveal a lighter side that fans rarely get to see. Kyle asks the trio how the idea for *Crown Season* came about and how Shane managed to bring it to life.

Mike points out that the EuroLeague has huge growth potential, especially if fans begin following not just their teams, but also the players they admire — regardless of which jersey they wear. The group discusses how the perception of players off the court has evolved, particularly after games, and they share a few funny stories along the way. From cultural differences to adapting to new environments, the conversation is relatable and engaging for any viewer.

Toward the end of the episode, Mike, Kyle, Mathias, and Shane debate the idea of a one‑on‑one contest — who should be invited, who would be the favorites, and what rules the competition should follow. Some of their proposed names are obvious, while others are surprising picks you might not expect. And the question remains: would Mike James volunteer himself… or nominate a Monaco teammate?

It’s rare to see four EuroLeague-winning stars openly discussing the league, the culture, and the players from their unique perspectives — so don’t miss *EuroLeague & Friends Global*!

articleMetadata.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season
articleMetadata.description: EuroLeague & Friends Global returns for its second episode, once again hosted by four‑time EuroLeague champion Kyle Hines. This time, the show dives into "Crown Season", a new docu-series created by Anadolu Efes Istanbul guard Shane Larkin. Kyle is joined by Larkin along with two of the series’ guest stars: Mike James, AS Monaco’s playmaker, and Mathias Lessort, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens’ center.
